what_it_does: Acceptance test for the RHM node's configurable model/provider, base_url, and persona. It builds a temporary FsStore-backed Suite, checks default config values, swaps and persists model/persona/base_url, verifies mode and config coexist on the same node, checks persistence across a new Suite session, and rejects unknown config keys.
observations: Inline docstring states the design rationale: no hardcoded default brain, any OpenAI-compatible provider, persona shapes voice, and config is persistent on the same RHM node.
Uses a temporary directory for the store and cleans it up in a finally block.
Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and prints per-check status.
Reads defaults via suite.rhm_config() and compares against fcfg.DEFAULT_BRAIN, DEFAULT_BASE_URL.
Sets config via suite.set_rhm_config(...) and reads it back.
Tests that mode (set_mode('focus')) and model config coexist without clobbering each other.
Tests persistence by creating a second Suite pointing at the same store path.
Tests key filtering by passing an unknown 'evil' key and asserting it is absent from s2._rhm_cfg().
standouts: Explicitly asserts DEFAULT_BRAIN is None so an unconfigured suite has no silent fallback model.
Explicitly rejects a Claude-locked provider by stating the fabric call is OpenAI-compatible and driven by model+base_url.
Includes an anti-injection test that unknown config keys are ignored.
Tests cross-session persistence by re-instantiating Suite on the same store directory.
conventions: Acceptance-test style with a custom assertion helper and global pass counter.
sys.path insertion to import from the project root.
Temporary store creation with shutil.rmtree cleanup.
Docstring explains product intent alongside test mechanics.
concerns: check mutates a global PASS counter, which can make parallel or imported execution brittle.
Persistence test creates a second FsStore on the same path without explicitly closing/flushing the first, which may be store-implementation-dependent.
The unknown-key test reaches into the private s2._rhm_cfg() method rather than using the public rhm_config() API.
The file claims the swap actually drives the fabric call, but the test only exercises the getter/setter, not a real chat invocation.
Tests depend on NODES directory and fabric.config constants that are not visible in this file.
notes: An acceptance test that encodes a policy: the RHM must have no silent default brain, configurable provider/persona, persistent config, and filtered config keys.
questions: What does RHM stand for in this codebase?
What node definitions exist under ROOT/nodes and how do they expose rhm_config/set_rhm_config/set_mode?
How does set_rhm_config filter unknown keys, and what is the full allowlist?
What value does fcfg.DEFAULT_BASE_URL have, and where is it defined?
How is the store flushed so that a second Suite sees persisted config reliably?
Is COMPANY_BRAIN tested elsewhere, and how does it interact with rhm_config()?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to prove config storage/resolution and that the swap drives the fabric call. The file proves storage, resolution, persistence, and key filtering, but does not actually exercise a fabric/chat call, so the 'swap driving the call' part is only asserted by docstring, not demonstrated.
apparent_themes: Separation of cognition model and persona voice
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce that the RHM brain is role-resolved and configurable, that the system is not tied to Claude, that persona is independent from model/provider, and that missing or bad configuration fails visibly rather than silently.
novelty: Explicitly tests that the default model is None as a first-class invariant, and includes an anti-injection config-key filter test, which is unusual for a simple acceptance test.
contribution: Provides acceptance-level coverage of the RHM configuration lifecycle: default values, mutation, cross-session persistence, coexistence with mode, and rejection of unknown keys, capturing the intended policy for configurable brains.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying RHM configuration defaults, model/provider/persona swaps, persistence across sessions, mode coexistence, and rejection of unknown config keys.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to prove the RHM's model, provider endpoint, and persona are configurable on the same node, persist, and do not silently fall back to a hardcoded default.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints per-check status.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition; on failure it raises an AssertionError with a 'FAIL: <label>' message, and on success it increments the global PASS counter and prints 'ok <label>'. It serves as the lightweight assertion primitive for this acceptance test file.
